evals.name: go-service
evals.prompt:
Create an AGENTS.md for a Go gRPC service called 'order-service'. Here's what you know about the repo:
- It has a Makefile with targets: build, test, test-all, lint, format, docker-build, run
- Go 1.22, uses protobuf/gRPC
- Key directories: cmd/server/, internal/handler/, internal/store/, proto/, e2e/
- CI: GitHub Actions runs lint + unit tests on PR, ArgoCD on merge
- Integration tests require a PostgreSQL database (docker-compose.yml exists)
- E2E tests require a running server + test database
- Security: handles PII data, has CODEOWNERS on internal/store/encryption/
- Conventions: conventional commits, golangci-lint, go fmt
- Single test pattern: go test -race -run TestName ./internal/path/...
Write the AGENTS.md to /tmp/eval-outputs/agents-go.md
evals.expected_output: A well-structured AGENTS.md covering project overview, commands, architecture, testing tiers, CI/CD, safety rails, and code conventions
expectations: The output contains a '## Commands' section with grouped subsections (Build, Test, Lint/Format)
The output documents the single-test pattern with go test -race -run under a dedicated 'Running a Single Test' heading or equivalent
The output has a dedicated '## Testing' section with distinct tiers (unit, integration, e2e) as separate items
The output has a '## Safety Rails' section with structured subsections like 'Forbidden Actions' or 'Requires Human Approval' mentioning the encryption directory
The output mentions that integration tests require PostgreSQL or docker-compose
The output documents code conventions including conventional commits
The output has a '## Project Overview' section as the first content section explaining what order-service is
The output mentions CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions and/or ArgoCD)
Commands are in fenced code blocks with inline comments explaining each command
The output is between 40 and 120 lines (concise but comprehensive)

evals.name: infra-repo
evals.prompt:
Create an AGENTS.md for a firmware-building repository. Here's the context:
- Repo name: edge-firmware
- Purpose: Builds firmware images for edge network appliances (gateways, routers) using Yocto recipes
- Build system: Makefile with targets: arm64, mips, x86_64, sign
- Build runs as root inside a Docker container on a dedicated build server
- No test suite — verification is done by flashing built images to reference hardware
- CI: One GitHub Action for image signature verification only
- Has 15 numbered runbook files in deploy/rollout/ for human-driven flashing campaigns
- Security-sensitive: changes to bootloader, partition layout, and signing keys are extremely dangerous
- Yocto recipes in build/recipes/ configure the entire firmware
- Shell scripts use set -euo pipefail
- Cross-repo dependency: images are validated by a separate hardware-test repo
Write the AGENTS.md to /tmp/eval-outputs/agents-infra.md
evals.expected_output: An infrastructure-focused AGENTS.md with heavy emphasis on safety rails, forbidden actions, credential handling, and cross-repo dependencies
expectations: The output has a '## Safety Rails' section with subsections for 'Forbidden Actions' and 'Requires Human Approval'
The safety section specifically lists bootloader, partition layout, and signing keys as items requiring human approval or forbidden from autonomous modification
The output documents build commands with make targets in a fenced code block
The output mentions that builds require root or privileged access in the Project Overview or Commands section
The output has a '## Cross-Repo Dependencies' section explaining the hardware-test relationship
The output notes that there is no test suite and verification is by flashing images to reference hardware
The output mentions the runbook files in deploy/rollout/ and notes they are for human-driven flashing
The output lists build/recipes/ as a sensitive path or warns about the sensitivity of Yocto recipes
The output includes a '## Project Overview' section as the first content section
The output documents the set -euo pipefail convention for shell scripts

evals.name: monorepo
evals.prompt:
Create AGENTS.md files for a TypeScript monorepo with this structure:
- Root: pnpm workspaces, Turborepo
- packages/api/ — Express REST API (Node 20, Jest tests)
- packages/web/ — Next.js 15 frontend (React 19, Vitest)
- packages/shared/ — Shared types and utilities (no tests yet)
- packages/cli/ — CLI tool (Commander.js, no tests yet)
- Root commands: pnpm install, pnpm turbo run test, pnpm turbo run lint, pnpm turbo run build
- Package-specific: pnpm --filter <pkg> test, pnpm --filter <pkg> dev
- CI: GitHub Actions matrix testing all packages
- Conventions: ESLint + Prettier, strict TypeScript, conventional commits
Write the root AGENTS.md to /tmp/eval-outputs/agents-monorepo-root.md and one nested AGENTS.md for the api package to /tmp/eval-outputs/agents-monorepo-api.md
evals.expected_output: A root AGENTS.md explaining the monorepo structure plus a nested AGENTS.md for the api package with package-specific details
expectations: Two separate files are created: one for root and one for the api package
The root file has a '## Project Overview' section explaining this is a monorepo
The root file lists all 4 packages with descriptions in an '## Architecture' section
The root file documents the --filter pattern for package-specific commands
The root file has a '## Testing' section that notes different test frameworks per package (Jest for api, Vitest for web)
The root file mentions that shared and cli packages lack tests
The root file mentions or explains nested AGENTS.md precedence rules
The api package file has api-specific commands including dev, test, and single-test pattern
The api package file mentions Express and Jest specifically
Both files are under 120 lines each (concise)

evals.name: update-stale
evals.prompt:
Update this existing AGENTS.md that has become stale. The repo migrated from Jest to Vitest, from yarn to pnpm, and added a new packages/mobile/ directory six months ago. None of these changes are reflected.

Current AGENTS.md content:
```
# AGENTS.md

## Commands
yarn install
yarn test          # Jest unit tests
yarn lint
yarn build

## Architecture
- packages/web/ — React frontend
- packages/api/ — Node backend
- packages/shared/ — Shared utils

## Conventions
- ESLint + Prettier
- yarn workspaces
```

New facts:
- pnpm replaced yarn (pnpm install, pnpm test, pnpm lint, pnpm build)
- Vitest replaced Jest (vitest run, vitest --watch)
- New package: packages/mobile/ — React Native app with Expo
- Added Turborepo: pnpm turbo run test, pnpm turbo run build
- New CI: GitHub Actions matrix across all 4 packages
Write the updated AGENTS.md to /tmp/eval-outputs/agents-updated.md
evals.expected_output: An updated AGENTS.md with all stale references replaced and new information added, plus structural enrichment
expectations: The output uses pnpm commands instead of yarn
The output references Vitest instead of Jest
The output includes packages/mobile/ in the architecture section
The output mentions Turborepo and turbo run commands
The output does NOT contain any yarn commands
The output does NOT reference Jest
The output mentions the CI matrix across all 4 packages
The output preserves the existing conventions section (ESLint + Prettier) while updating workspace reference
The output adds a '## Project Overview' section that wasn't in the original (enrichment beyond migration)
The output adds a dedicated '## Testing' or '## CI/CD' section that wasn't in the original
